summary: First iteration end-to-end test of AI Listing Studio. Backend pytest suite (19 tests) 100% pass covering auth, products CRUD, AI generation & regeneration, listing edits, stats, uploads (CSV), bulk generate-all, xlsx exports (amazon/flipkart/generic), and settings. Frontend Playwright smoke test verified landing (hero, sign-in, theme toggle), /login (google button), auth-gated dashboard with 4 stat cards + recent products table + search/filter, AddProduct save-draft + generate flow, ProductDetails tabs (info/amazon/flipkart/seo/history) plus copy/regen/save actions, sidebar nav to all 5 pages, Exports page with 3 export buttons, Settings save, Bulk Upload dropzone. All data-testid attributes present.

issues: 'Sign in' (landing-login-btn) bypasses /login route and directly launches Google OAuth. Spec said it should route to /login. Not a blocker since /login exists and hero-cta-btn also goes directly to Google.

action_items: (Optional/nit) Consider routing landing-login-btn to /login instead of directly to Google OAuth to match spec; or update spec. Not a functional bug.

(Optional/nit) On ProductDetails page, when clicking a sidebar NavLink while a section save/regen mutation is in-flight, playwright observed the navigation appeared delayed (works with slightly longer wait). Consider ensuring pending fetches don't block RAF; not a real bug in production.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: create_product accepts status query param but silently coerces 'exported' to 'draft'. Consider 400 for invalid status.
server.py update_listing: pops '_id' but does not validate payload keys against a schema; any client can add arbitrary fields to generated_listings docs.
server.py generate_all: bulk-inserts products with status='completed' but does not de-duplicate rows or validate; large uploads could blow up memory (rows kept in doc). Not blocking for MVP.
ai_service.regenerate_section: appends whitespace 'salt' which is invisible; real regeneration should call provider with slight prompt variation, but acceptable for mock.
test_credentials: Bearer test_session_fixed_123 / cookie session_token=test_session_fixed_123 (user_id test-user-1, email qa@example.com). Seeded via mongosh per /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Seeded test-user-1 + session token in test_database. Test artifacts (TEST_* products, listings, uploads, exports) cleaned up at end of run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run with `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. It self-cleans TEST_ artefacts. Auth: Bearer test_session_fixed_123. For Playwright, seed cookie {name:'session_token', value:'test_session_fixed_123', domain:'commerce-ai-craft.preview.emergentagent.com', path:'/', httpOnly:true, secure:true, sameSite:'None'} then navigate to /dashboard directly. Do NOT click the landing 'Sign in' button — it goes to real Google OAuth. AI provider is a MOCK per requirements (not a bug).
rca of the issue: No blocking issues. Minor spec deviation on landing Sign-in button routing directly to Google instead of /login is intentional-looking UX; /login route still functions and is reachable via ProtectedRoute redirects.
